releases.title: collectZ v3.4.161
releases.summary: This patch makes scanner barcode imports run the existing backend enrichment pipeline before creating or updating the canonical media row.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: `POST /api/media/import-barcode` now enriches the selected scanner match with available ISBN, UPC, title, and provider data before upsert.
ISBN book imports can populate Books/Google Books metadata such as overview, cover image, publisher, and type details.
Import responses now include `enrichment_status`, `lookup_path`, and `lookup_status` so scanner clients can show what happened.
Barcode import activity and metadata record the scanner enrichment outcome for readback/debugging.
Scanner smoke now proves ISBN import enrichment end-to-end with a fake Books provider.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.160
releases.summary: This patch makes ISBN-shaped barcode scans use the configured Books provider directly, so the iOS scanner app can get Google Books-style candidates without routing through the web UI or forcing the client to know the media type first.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Scanner barcode lookup now detects ISBN-10/ISBN-13 shaped codes automatically.
Web UPC lookup now also uses the direct ISBN path when a scanned identifier normalizes to ISBN.
Direct ISBN matches return with `source: books:isbn-direct` and `mediaTypeGuess: book`.
Scanner smoke now proves the ISBN path works without `mediaType: book`.
OpenAPI now documents scanner ISBN behavior on `/api/media/lookup/barcode`.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.159
releases.summary: This patch surfaces distinct barcode provider title variants from nested offer/listing rows so both the web UPC lookup and iOS scanner lookup can show messy real-world UPC choices like `Before the Storm` when the provider includes that title beneath a top-level product.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Expanded normalized barcode matches to include distinct `offers[].title` and listing title variants.
Kept the top-level provider product candidate while adding meaningful provider title variants as additional candidates.
Added `alternateTitles`/`alternate_titles` readback so clients can show related provider title strings.
Added title-variant metadata fields such as `titleVariantSource`, `titleVariantIndex`, and `offerIndex`.
Updated scanner smoke coverage so both `/api/media/lookup/barcode` and `/api/media/lookup-upc` prove offer title variants are returned.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.158
releases.summary: This patch keeps scanner barcode lookup as a candidate search by preserving multiple provider products for a single barcode instead of allowing duplicate provider identifiers to collapse choices in scanner clients.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Preserved multiple barcode provider matches in `POST /api/media/lookup/barcode`.
Added stable per-candidate provider IDs so barcode variants sharing the same UPC stay distinct.
Added `provider_candidate_index` to scanner lookup matches when provider ordering is available.
Updated scanner smoke coverage with a fake UPC provider returning three candidates for `0076783005990`.
Clarified OpenAPI docs that barcode lookup may return zero, one, or many candidates.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.157
releases.summary: This patch adds the backend scanner contract needed for the iOS barcode scanner app to look up decoded barcodes and import selected matches without routing through the web UI.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added authenticated `POST /api/media/lookup/barcode` for scanner clients.
The scanner lookup reads scoped existing catalog matches first, then configured barcode/provider enrichment matches when available.
Empty barcode lookups now return a successful response with an empty `matches` array.
Added authenticated `POST /api/media/import-barcode` to create or update canonical media from a selected scanner match.
Added PAT/service-token scope mapping so scanner lookup uses `media:read` and barcode import uses the existing `import:run` import scope.
Updated OpenAPI and smoke coverage for lookup success, empty lookup, import success, and auth behavior.
Follow-up `3.4.158` keeps this scanner lookup contract as a true candidate list when a provider returns multiple products for one barcode.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.156
releases.summary: Kavita chapter-as-issue rows now use a chapter-specific cover proxy instead of repeating the parent series cover for every imported issue.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a scoped `/api/media/kavita-chapter-cover/{chapterId}` proxy that fetches Kavita reader page `0` server-side.
Updated Kavita chapter fan-out rows so `poster_path` points at the chapter-cover proxy.
Added a data repair migration for existing Kavita chapter rows that still pointed at the parent series cover proxy.
Kept series rows on the existing series-cover proxy.
Updated Kavita docs and smoke coverage for chapter-level issue cover behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.155
releases.summary: Kavita comic libraries that report numeric library type `5` now qualify for comic chapter issue fan-out. This fixes imports that were correctly classified as `comic_book` rows but still skipped all chapter fan-out as if they were books.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.154
releases.summary: Kavita comic imports now lean toward showing the issues Kavita exposes, not quietly stopping at the parent series row. The admin Kavita import control defaults chapter issue fan-out on, special chapters import as issue rows, and chapter rows can be kept when Kavita provides sort/order metadata even if the usual issue fields are sparse.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.153
releases.summary: This patch improves Kavita comic imports when Kavita exposes an issue-shaped comic file as a series row. collectZ now recognizes titles such as `Alpha Flight#130 - The Hollow Man! - Unknown`, keeps the raw Kavita title for troubleshooting, and stores cleaner comic series and issue metadata.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Normalizes issue-like Kavita comic series titles into cleaner display titles, series names, issue numbers, and issue titles.
Keeps true Kavita comic series rows at the series level instead of copying first-chapter issue number and cover date onto the series row.
Prevents chapter fan-out from creating a duplicate issue row when the Kavita series row already represents an issue-like comic.
Preserves raw Kavita series/localized/original/sort names plus parse status metadata for troubleshooting.
Extends the Kavita import smoke fixture with the real `Alpha Flight#130 - The Hollow Man! - Unknown` pattern.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.152
releases.summary: This patch adds reusable artist records to Art entry. Artwork can still keep its own simple artist text, but users can now create an artist once, reuse that record across multiple works, and see linked artist details from the artwork detail view.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Adds reusable Art artist records with names, sort names, aliases, website links, notes, and linked-work counts.
Adds `artist_id` and `artist_role` to Art rows so each artwork can link to an artist while keeping item-specific text and role metadata.
Adds typeahead artist lookup and inline artist creation inside the Art drawer.
Shows linked artist metadata on Art detail with a shortcut to view other works by the same artist.
Documents and tests the new Art artist API in OpenAPI, migration/init parity, source checks, and browser regression coverage.
